docs.title: fluxnova-bpm-platform
docs.text:
Fluxnova BPMN Platform - LinkML Schema

  • Auto-generated schema documentation

docs.title: About fluxnova-bpm-platform
docs.text:
A LinkML data model for the Fluxnova BPM Platform - the process engine's persistence schema and its BPMN 2.0 model API, expressed as one coherent, machine-validatable schema.

The schema is generated, not hand-written: transformer scripts read the engine's own sources (H2 DDL, Java interface Javadoc, MyBatis mappings) and produce LinkML YAML that you can validate data against, render as docs, or project into JSON Schema, Python, OWL, and more. Re-running the generators on unchanged inputs produces byte-identical output.

docs.title: At a glance
docs.text:
• Two domains in one model - runtime/persistence entities (from the database schema) and the BPMN 2.0 model API (from Java interfaces).
  • Platform side: 49 SQL tables -> 51 classes, 192 slots, 7 enums (100% of tables mapped).
  • BPMN side: 181 Java interfaces -> 6 modular schemas, 10 enums, 28 Fluxnova extension classes.
  • Provenance overlay - a W3C PROV-aligned record of workflow definitions, runs, and per-invocation AI activity.
  • Ontology cross-walks - 26 SSSOM mapping files (~4,450 element mappings) to standards like ATT&CK, D3FEND, OCSF, NIST, and the FINOS CDM.
  • 419 tests, all passing, plus reproducible regeneration and linkml-lint clean.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Source: github.com/finos/fluxnova-bpm-platform
  • Project home: fluxnova.finos.org

docs.title: Quality gates
docs.text:
Every regeneration is checked against these gates:

Gate Status linkml-lint ✅ No problems found gen-json-schema ✅ Pass gen-python ✅ Pass Reproducibility (identical output on re-run) ✅ Pass SSSOM overlay (overlay-sssom-mappings) ✅ Pass (idempotent, 26 mapping files loaded)

docs.title: What's in the schema
docs.text: The model is split into small, focused modules. A handful of root schemas import the modules to give you a single entry point per domain.

docs.title: Platform modules (persistence & runtime)
docs.text:
Generated from the H2 DDL - one module per functional area:

Module Classes Highlights fluxnova_bpm_base 5 ByteArray, MeterLog, Property, SchemaLogEntry, TaskMeterLog (+ 7 enums) fluxnova_bpm_repository 7 ResourceDefinition (abstract), ProcessDefinition, CaseDefinition, DecisionDefinition, DecisionRequirementsDefinition, FormDefinition, Deployment fluxnova_bpm_runtime 8 Execution, Task, VariableInstance, EventSubscription, Incident, ExternalTask, CaseExecution, CaseSentryPart fluxnova_bpm_job 3 Job, JobDefinition, Batch fluxnova_bpm_identity 8 User, Group, Tenant, Membership, TenantMembership, IdentityLink, IdentityInfo, Authorization fluxnova_bpm_history 17 Historic* snapshots (process, activity, task, variable, decision, case, …) + UserOperationLogEntry fluxnova_bpm_collaboration 3 Comment, Attachment, Filter

docs.title: BPMN model modules
docs.text:
Generated from the public BPMN Model API Java interfaces:

Module Classes Covers fluxnova_bpmn_model_dc 3 Diagram Common (Bounds, Font, Point) fluxnova_bpmn_model_di 12 Diagram Interchange (Diagram, Edge, Plane, Shape, Style, Label, …) fluxnova_bpmn_model_instance 130 Core BPMN 2.0 elements (Process, Activity, Task, Gateway, Event, Data objects, …) fluxnova_bpmn_model_bpmndi 6 BPMN Diagram Interchange (BpmnDiagram, BpmnPlane, BpmnShape, BpmnEdge, BpmnLabel, BpmnLabelStyle) fluxnova_bpmn_model_fluxnova 28 Fluxnova extensions (FluxnovaConnector, FluxnovaFormField, FluxnovaListener, …)

The BPMN modules import each other in layers (dc -> di; instance -> di; bpmndi -> di + dc; fluxnova_extensions -> instance), with cross-module types that would create cycles flattened to range: string. Slot descriptions come from a mix of curated fallbacks and automatic pattern inference (~300 of them).

docs.title: Root schemas
docs.text:
These tie everything together:

Schema Local classes Role fluxnova_common 0 (slots only) Shared slot definitions (id, tenant_id, revision, …) imported by every module so the same concept never gets two conflicting URIs. fluxnova_bpm_platform 1 The top-level entry point: imports fluxnova_common, all platform modules, fluxnova_bpmn_model, and the provenance overlay. Defines FluxnovaPlatformData as the tree root. fluxnova_bpmn_model 2 BPMN entry point: imports all BPMN modules. Defines BpmnModelInstance and BpmnModelType.

docs.title: Provenance
docs.text:
A hand-authored, W3C PROV-aligned provenance overlay lives under src/fluxnova_bpm_platform/schema/provenance/ (base + per-invocation AI overlay + opt-in strict profile). It is not derived from the H2 DDL; the platform root imports it so ProvenanceBundle and friends survive regeneration.

See elements/provenance/README.md for the module breakdown, mapping spec, RO-Crate/JSON-LD projection, AI overlay overview, fixtures, and the SSSOM cross-walk to the FINOS AI Governance Framework (nexus:).

docs.title: Enums
docs.text:
Platform:

Enum Values SuspensionState ACTIVE, SUSPENDED DelegationState PENDING, RESOLVED ActivityInstanceState DEFAULT, SCOPE_COMPLETE, CANCELED, STARTING, ENDING IncidentState OPEN, DELETED, RESOLVED JobState CREATED, FAILED, SUCCESSFUL, DELETED EntityState ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, COMPLETED, EXTERNALLY_TERMINATED, INTERNALLY_TERMINATED AuthorizationType GLOBAL, GRANT, REVOKE

BPMN model:

Enum Values AssociationDirection None, One, Both EventBasedGatewayType Exclusive, Parallel GatewayDirection Unspecified, Converging, Diverging, Mixed ItemKind Information, Physical MessageVisibleKind initiating, non_initiating MultiInstanceFlowCondition None, One, All, Complex ParticipantBandKind top_initiating, middle_initiating, bottom_initiating, top_non_initiating, middle_non_initiating, bottom_non_initiating ProcessType None, Public, Private RelationshipDirection None, Forward, Backward, Both TransactionMethod Compensate, Image, Store

docs.title: Subsets (filtered views)
docs.text:
Every class is tagged with subsets so you can carve out domain-specific views or docs. Tagging is automatic and deterministic - derived from the file a class lives in - and enforced by a unit test, so it never drifts.

Each class carries two subsets: its module subset and a cross-cutting model subset (fluxnova_bpm or fluxnova_bpmn_model). For example, ByteArray -> in_subset: [base, fluxnova_bpm].

Platform subset Contents base Base types and utility entities (ByteArray, Property, Meter, Schema). repository Resource definitions (Process, Case, Decision, Form). runtime Active execution, task, and event instances. job Job and batch management entities. identity Identity and authorization entities (User, Group, Tenant, Role). history Historical snapshots of completed runtime entities. collaboration Collaboration features (Comments, Attachments, Filters). fluxnova_bpm Cross-cutting: all Fluxnova BPM platform entities. BPMN subset Contents core_api Top-level BPMN API types and enums. instance Core BPMN element interfaces. bpmndi BPMN diagram interchange interfaces and enums. di Generic diagram interchange interfaces. dc Diagram coordinate and bounds interfaces. fluxnova_extensions Fluxnova BPMN extension interfaces.

docs.title: Source data
docs.text:
The platform transformer reads the engine's H2 DDL - 7 SQL files in engine/src/main/resources/org/finos/fluxnova/bpm/engine/db/create/:

File Tables activiti.h2.create.engine.sql Core runtime and repository activiti.h2.create.identity.sql Identity management activiti.h2.create.history.sql History activiti.h2.create.case.engine.sql CMMN case runtime activiti.h2.create.case.history.sql CMMN case history activiti.h2.create.decision.engine.sql DMN decision activiti.h2.create.decision.history.sql DMN decision history

The BPMN transformer reads the public Java interfaces and enums under model-api/bpmn-model/.

docs.title: The transformer scripts
docs.text:
The maintained workflow lives in four scripts:

  • fluxnova_to_linkml.py - builds the platform schemas from H2 DDL, Java Javadoc, and MyBatis inputs. It adds subset tags, embeds the SQL column name on every slot, emits the shared fluxnova_common.yaml, and fills in descriptions from Javadoc (with sensible fallbacks).

  • fluxnova_bpmn_model_to_linkml.py - builds the modular BPMN model schemas from the public Java interfaces, with package-level subsets and curated slot descriptions.

  • fluxnova_mybatis_enrichment.py - a cross-check, not a generator: it parses the 55 MyBatis XML mappers (336 field->column mappings) and confirms they line up with the DDL-derived slot names. It writes nothing and exits non-zero if anything disagrees.

  • overlay_sssom.py - applies the SSSOM ontology mappings onto the generated YAML (see SSSOM mappings below). It runs automatically after each generation step.

docs.title: SQL column annotations
docs.text:
Every slot generated from the DDL records the database column it came from, right in the schema - no sidecar mapping file needed:

slots:
  exception_message:
    range: string
    description: Message of the exception that occurred.
    annotations:
      sql_column: EXCEPTION_MSG_

The SQL column name is the ground truth; the slot name is its LinkML-idiomatic snake_case form. Consumers read annotations.sql_column to map a slot back to its database column. The fluxnova_mybatis_enrichment.py cross-check keeps these honest against the MyBatis ORM mappings:

python scripts/fluxnova_mybatis_enrichment.py            # pass/fail check
python scripts/fluxnova_mybatis_enrichment.py --verbose  # show all field mappings

docs.title: SSSOM mappings
docs.text:
Cross-walks to external ontologies and standards are kept as SSSOM v0.9 TSV files in src/fluxnova_bpm_platform/mappings/. overlay_sssom.py reads them and injects *_mappings and prefixes entries into the generated schema YAML - in sorted, deduplicated, comment-preserving, idempotent fashion.

Coverage - 26 mapping files (~4,451 element mappings):

File Rows Covers fluxnova-attack.sssom.tsv 325 MITRE ATT&CK fluxnova-capec.sssom.tsv 14 MITRE CAPEC fluxnova-cis-controls.sssom.tsv 37 CIS Controls v8 fluxnova-common-domain-model.sssom.tsv 659 FINOS CDM (merged from 23 sub-schemas) fluxnova-cra.sssom.tsv 99 EU Cyber Resilience Act fluxnova-cve.sssom.tsv 61 MITRE CVE fluxnova-cwe.sssom.tsv 25 MITRE CWE fluxnova-cyberinfra.sssom.tsv 8 Cyber Infrastructure fluxnova-d3fend.sssom.tsv 7,523 MITRE D3FEND fluxnova-generic.sssom.tsv 1,530 Generic/cross-cutting concepts fluxnova-gist.sssom.tsv 40 Semantic Arts GIST (merged from 4 sub-schemas) fluxnova-iso27001.sssom.tsv 14 ISO/IEC 27001 fluxnova-iso29100.sssom.tsv 58 ISO/IEC 29100 (Privacy) fluxnova-kev-catalog.sssom.tsv 58 CISA KEV Catalog fluxnova-mcp.sssom.tsv 10 Model Context Protocol fluxnova-nist-csf-v2.sssom.tsv 22 NIST CSF v2 fluxnova-nist-nvd.sssom.tsv 63 NIST NVD fluxnova-nist-sp-800-171.sssom.tsv 10 NIST SP 800-171 fluxnova-nist-sp-800-218.sssom.tsv 10 NIST SP 800-218 (SSDF) fluxnova-nist-sp-800-53.sssom.tsv 10 NIST SP 800-53 fluxnova-ocsf.sssom.tsv 124 OCSF (merged from 4 sub-schemas) fluxnova-oscal.sssom.tsv 10 NIST OSCAL fluxnova-slsa.sssom.tsv 10 SLSA Supply-Chain Security fluxnova-spdx.sssom.tsv 10 SPDX (Software Package Data Exchange) fluxnova-stix.sssom.tsv 58 OASIS STIX 2.1 fluxnova-vulnerability-core.sssom.tsv 30 Vulnerability Core ontology

Key namespaces:

Prefix URI common_domain_model: https://w3id.org/lmodel/common-domain-model/ gist: https://w3id.org/lmodel/gist/ ocsf: https://w3id.org/lmodel/ocsf/ d3f: https://d3fend.mitre.org/ontologies/d3fend.owl# attack: https://w3id.org/lmodel/attack/

SSSOM predicates map onto LinkML mapping slots as follows:

SSSOM predicate LinkML field skos:exactMatch exact_mappings skos:closeMatch close_mappings skos:broadMatch broad_mappings skos:narrowMatch narrow_mappings skos:relatedMatch related_mappings

docs.title: Regenerating
docs.text:
The overlay step runs automatically after each generation recipe, so you rarely call it by hand.

# Everything (BPMN model + platform), then overlay mappings
just gen-all

# Just the platform schemas (from H2 DDL)
just gen-linkml
just gen-linkml --dry-run        # report only, write nothing

# Just the BPMN model schemas (from Java interfaces)
python scripts/fluxnova_bpmn_model_to_linkml.py

# Re-apply SSSOM mappings without regenerating
just overlay-sssom-mappings
just overlay-sssom-mappings --dry-run --verbose

docs.title: Validating & testing
docs.text:
# Lint the schema
just lint-schema

# Full reproducibility gate: regenerate -> lint -> cross-validate -> test
just verify-schema

# MyBatis ↔ DDL field-name cross-check
just validate-mybatis

# Run the test suite
just test
# or directly:
pytest tests/ -v

docs.title: Test coverage
docs.text:
419 tests, all passing:

Test module Tests What it checks tests/test_fluxnova_to_linkml.py 97 Platform transformer (parsing, mapping, generation) tests/test_schema.py 20 Schema-level lint, generators, structure tests/test_data.py 187 Valid/invalid example data against the schema (covers BPM, BPMN, and provenance classes) tests/test_ddl_coverage.py 104 DDL cross-validation: table coverage, column->slot, vendor files tests/test_bpmn_model_schema.py 4 BPMN schema validation, lint, JSON Schema generation tests/test_fluxnova_bpmn_model_to_linkml.py 7 BPMN transformer (discovery, headers, coverage, inheritance, serialization)

The example data is realistic: 33 of the valid files (tests/data/valid/*-vendor.yaml) reuse the exact constants the upstream engine uses in its own REST API tests (engine-rest/.../helper/MockProvider.java). The invalid files deliberately break things - missing required fields, bad enum values, wrong types - to prove validation rejects them. A few cross-schema cases (Point-001.yaml, Bounds-001.yaml, Font-001.yaml, and their bad counterparts) confirm the platform root correctly validates imported BPMN classes.

docs.title: What's intentionally out of scope
docs.text:
• REST DTOs (engine-rest/) - API transfer objects that mirror persistence entities; excluded to avoid duplication.
  • Java implementation entity classes (engine/.../impl/.../entity) - a review of representative entities (ExecutionEntity, TaskEntity, CamundaFormDefinitionEntity, timer/job subclasses) found no DDL-backed surface beyond the generated model. Their extra fields are transient caches, runtime helpers, or subtype behavior already covered by existing tables.

ProspectiveProvenance Provenance entities describing what a workflow is (definitions and contracts) Repository Resource definitions (Process, Case, Decision, Form) RetrospectiveProvenance Provenance entities describing what a workflow did (runs, observed values, in... Runtime Active execution, task, and event instances

docs.title: Enum: AgentKind
docs.text: Kind of agent participating in workflow execution.

docs.title: Class: AiAgent
docs.text:
Autonomous or semi-autonomous AI agent that acts as an Agent in a Fluxnova

workflow. Concrete embodiment of one or more AI models exposed through a

provider endpoint. Distinct from the human, organizational, or system Agent

kinds declared in the base provenance module.

docs.title: Induced
docs.text:
name: AiAgent
description: 'Autonomous or semi-autonomous AI agent that acts as an Agent in a Fluxnova

  workflow. Concrete embodiment of one or more AI models exposed through a

  provider endpoint. Distinct from the human, organizational, or system Agent

  kinds declared in the base provenance module.'
in_subset:
- ai_prospective
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
exact_mappings:
- airo:AIAgent
- nexus:AiAgent
- prov:SoftwareAgent
related_mappings:
- dpv_ai:AISystem
is_a: Agent
attributes:
  model_family:
    name: model_family
    description: Model family or product line (e.g. "GPT-4", "Claude 3", "Llama 3",
      "Mistral 7B").
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:productID
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - AiAgent
    - AiModelDescriptor
    range: string
  model_name:
    name: model_name
    description: Concrete model name (e.g. "gpt-4o-2024-05-13", "claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620").
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:name
    owner: AiAgent

    - AiAgent
    range: string

    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:softwareVersion
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - AiAgent
    range: string
  provider:
    name: provider
    description: 'Provider or vendor that hosts the model endpoint (e.g. "openai",
      "anthropic",

      "azure-openai", "vertex-ai", "self-hosted-vllm").'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - AiAgent
    range: string
  endpoint:
    name: endpoint
    description: API or service endpoint URL.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform

    - ExecutionResource

    exact_mappings:
    - nexus:numParameters
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - AiAgent
    - AiModelDescriptor
    - ModelArtifact
    range: integer
  context_window_tokens:
    name: context_window_tokens
    description: Maximum input context window in tokens.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    exact_mappings:
    - nexus:contextWindowSize
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - AiAgent
    - AiModelDescriptor
    range: integer
  input_modalities:
    name: input_modalities
    description: Modalities the model accepts as input.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    exact_mappings:
    - nexus:hasInputModality
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - AiAgent
    range: AiModality
    multivalued: true

    description: Modalities the model produces as output.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    exact_mappings:

    rank: 1000
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - AiAgent
    range: AiModality
    multivalued: true

    name: is_part_of_ai_system
    description: 'External CURIE or URI reference to a larger AI system this agent
      belongs

      to (typically modeled in an AI governance vocabulary such as nexus:AiSystem

      or airo:AISystem). String reference rather than typed slot so the AI

      provenance overlay does not pull in a governance ontology.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    related_mappings:
    - airo:AISystem
    - nexus:AiSystem
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - AiAgent
    range: string
  agent_kind:
    name: agent_kind
    description: Kind of agent (person, organization, system, service account).
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiAgent
    domain_of:
    - Agent
    range: AgentKind
  email:
    name: email
    annotations:
      sql_column:
        tag: sql_column
        value: EMAIL_
    description: Email address.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000

docs.title: Class: AiDataset
docs.text:
Dataset artifact associated with an AI model invocation, an evaluation,

a training run, or a fine-tuning step. Extends the generic WorkflowArtifact

class with AI-specific metadata such as dataset kind, record count, license,

and data classification.

docs.title: Class: AiModelDescriptor
docs.text:
Descriptor of an AI model used as a runtime component. Captures static

characteristics of the model (family, architecture, parameter count,

training tokens, context window, supported languages) without prescribing

how an individual call was made.

docs.title: Class: AiModelInvocation
docs.text:
One concrete invocation of an AI model from a workflow step. Captures the

runtime call (kind, parameters, prompts, tool calls, response), the

consumption telemetry (token counts, cost), any safety flags raised, and

any evaluation results attached. Inherits status, timestamps, parent

workflow run, sequence number, and incident linkage from StepRun.

docs.title: Induced
docs.text:
name: AiModelInvocation
description: 'One concrete invocation of an AI model from a workflow step. Captures
  the

  runtime call (kind, parameters, prompts, tool calls, response), the

  consumption telemetry (token counts, cost), any safety flags raised, and

  any evaluation results attached. Inherits status, timestamps, parent

  workflow run, sequence number, and incident linkage from StepRun.'
in_subset:
- ai_retrospective
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
exact_mappings:
- prov:Activity
- schema:CreateAction
is_a: StepRun
attributes:
  invoked_model:
    name: invoked_model
    description: AI agent that was invoked to produce the step result.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    exact_mappings:
    - prov:wasAssociatedWith
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: prov:wasAssociatedWith
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: AiAgent
  invocation_kind:
    name: invocation_kind
    description: Kind of invocation (chat completion, tool use, embedding, etc.).
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: AiInvocationKind
  prompt_messages:

    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: PromptMessage
    multivalued: true
    inlined: true
    inlined_as_list: true
  tool_calls:
    name: tool_calls
    description: Tool / function calls performed by the model during the invocation.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: ToolCall
    multivalued: true
    inlined: true
    inlined_as_list: true
  response_message:
    name: response_message
    description: 'Assistant or tool message returned by the model as the response.

      Inlined as a single PromptMessage with role ASSISTANT or TOOL.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: PromptMessage
  temperature:

    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: decimal

    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform

    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: decimal
  max_tokens:

    description: Maximum tokens the model was permitted to generate.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: integer
  total_input_tokens:
    name: total_input_tokens
    description: Total prompt / input tokens consumed by the invocation.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: integer
  total_output_tokens:
    name: total_output_tokens
    description: Total completion / output tokens produced by the invocation.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: integer
  cost_usd:
    name: cost_usd
    description: 'Direct provider cost in USD for the invocation, where reported by
      the

      provider or computed locally from billing rates.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: decimal
  safety_flags:
    name: safety_flags
    description: 'Safety, policy, or guardrail flags raised by the model, provider,
      or

      local guardrail layer during the invocation.'
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: SafetyFlag
    multivalued: true
  evaluation_results:
    name: evaluation_results
    description: Evaluation results attached to the invocation.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - AiModelInvocation
    range: EvaluationResult
    multivalued: true
    inlined: true
    inlined_as_list: true
  workflow_run:
    name: workflow_run
    description: Parent workflow run for the step run.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - StepRun
    range: WorkflowRun

    name: step_definition
    description: Step definition this run instantiates.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:instrument
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - StepRun
    range: StepDefinition
  activity_instance_id:
    name: activity_instance_id
    description: Runtime activity instance identifier.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation

    - Execution
    - ExternalTask
    - HistoricDecisionInstance
    - HistoricDetail
    - HistoricExternalTaskLog
    - HistoricTaskInstance
    - HistoricVariableInstance
    - StepRun
    range: string
  parent_step_run:
    name: parent_step_run
    description: Parent step run for nested step executions.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - StepRun
    range: StepRun
  informed_by:
    name: informed_by
    description: Step runs whose completion preceded and informed this step run (by-reference).
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: prov:wasInformedBy
    owner: AiModelInvocation
    domain_of:
    - StepRun
    range: StepRun
    multivalued: true

docs.title: Class: EvaluationResult
docs.text:
Result of a quality or safety evaluation against an AI model invocation.

Supports human review, LLM-as-judge, deterministic metrics, guardrails,

and static rule checks. Distinct from offline benchmark scoring such as

nexus:AiEvalResult (a related but coarser concept).

docs.title: Induced

  Supports human review, LLM-as-judge, deterministic metrics, guardrails,

  and static rule checks. Distinct from offline benchmark scoring such as

  nexus:AiEvalResult (a related but coarser concept).'

docs.title: Jsonpath
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Jsonpath

A string encoding a JSON Path. The value of the string MUST conform to JSON Point syntax and SHOULD dereference to zero or more valid objects within the current instance document when encoded in tree form.

docs.title: Jsonpointer
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Jsonpointer

A string encoding a JSON Pointer. The value of the string MUST conform to JSON Point syntax and SHOULD dereference to a valid object within the current instance document when encoded in tree form.

docs.title: Class: PromptMessage
docs.text:
A single message turn in an AI model invocation (system, user, assistant,

or tool turn). Records the role, the (possibly redacted) content, an

ordering index inside the invocation, and a data classification.

docs.title: Enum: RuntimeComponentKind

docs.title: Enum: SafetyFlag
docs.text:
Safety, policy, or guardrail flag raised by the model, the provider, or

a local guardrail layer during an AI model invocation. Mapped to

dpv-risk terms where a stable upstream match exists.

search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Sparqlpath

docs.title: Class: ToolCall
docs.text:
A single tool / function call made by an AI agent during an invocation.

Captures the tool name, the provider-assigned call identifier, the

arguments passed, the result returned, the status of the call, and an

optional link to the step run that materialized the call.

docs.title: Class: WorkflowRun
docs.text: Concrete execution of a WorkflowDefinition (retrospective provenance).

docs.title: Induced
docs.text:
name: WorkflowRun
description: Concrete execution of a WorkflowDefinition (retrospective provenance).
in_subset:
- retrospective_provenance
from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
exact_mappings:
- prov:Activity
- schema:CreateAction
- fluxnova_bpm_history:HistoricProcessInstance
is_a: NamedThing
attributes:
  workflow_definition:
    name: workflow_definition
    description: Reference to the owning workflow definition.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:instrument
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - StepDefinition
    - WorkflowRun
    range: WorkflowDefinition
  business_key:
    name: business_key
    description: Domain-specific business key.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - CaseExecution
    - Execution
    - HistoricCaseInstance
    - HistoricProcessInstance
    - WorkflowRun
    range: string
  parent_workflow_run:
    name: parent_workflow_run
    description: Parent workflow run when the run is a sub-process invocation.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowRun
    range: WorkflowRun
  status:
    name: status
    description: Status of the workflow run or step run.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    related_mappings:
    - core:status
    - nvd:NVDWorkflowStatus
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:actionStatus
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - ToolCall
    - WorkflowRun
    - StepRun
    range: RunStatus
  started_at:
    name: started_at
    description: Timestamp when execution started.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:startTime
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - ToolCall
    - WorkflowRun
    - StepRun
    range: datetime
  ended_at:
    name: ended_at
    description: Timestamp when execution ended (absent for running instances).
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:endTime
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - ToolCall
    - WorkflowRun
    - StepRun
    range: datetime
  started_by:
    name: started_by
    description: Agent that initiated the workflow run.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    narrow_mappings:
    - schema:agent
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: prov:wasAssociatedWith
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowRun
    range: Agent
  environment:
    name: environment
    description: Runtime environment in which the run took place.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowRun
    - StepRun
    range: Environment
  runtime_component:
    name: runtime_component
    description: Engine or runtime component associated with the entity.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:instrument
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowDefinition
    - WorkflowRun
    range: RuntimeComponent
  input_values:
    name: input_values
    description: Observed input parameter values at the workflow or step boundary
      (by-reference; declare full values under ProvenanceBundle.parameter_values).
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:object
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowRun
    - StepRun
    range: ParameterValue
    multivalued: true
  output_values:
    name: output_values
    description: Observed output parameter values at the workflow or step boundary
      (by-reference; declare full values under ProvenanceBundle.parameter_values).
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:result
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowRun
    - StepRun
    range: ParameterValue
    multivalued: true
  step_runs:
    name: step_runs
    description: Step run executions belonging to the workflow run.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowRun
    range: StepRun
    multivalued: true
    inlined: true
    inlined_as_list: true
  incidents:
    name: incidents
    description: Incidents recorded against the workflow run.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowRun
    range: ProvenanceIncident
    multivalued: true
    inlined: true
    inlined_as_list: true
  resource_usage:
    name: resource_usage
    description: Resource usage measurements for the run.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowRun
    range: ResourceUsage
    multivalued: true
    inlined: true
    inlined_as_list: true
  tags:
    name: tags
    description: Free-form tag labels.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    rank: 1000
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - WorkflowDefinition
    - WorkflowRun
    range: string
    multivalued: true
  id:
    name: id
    description: Unique identifier.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    related_mappings:
    - cis_controls:id
    - nist_csf_v2:id
    - stix:id
    - unified_cyber_ontology:externalReference
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:identifier
    identifier: true
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - ByteArray
    - MeterLog
    - SchemaLogEntry
    - TaskMeterLog
    - Authorization
    - Group
    - IdentityInfo
    - IdentityLink
    - Tenant
    - TenantMembership
    - User
    - CaseExecution
    - CaseSentryPart
    - EventSubscription
    - Execution
    - ExternalTask
    - Incident
    - Task
    - VariableInstance
    - Attachment
    - Comment
    - Filter
    - Deployment
    - ResourceDefinition
    - Batch
    - Job
    - JobDefinition
    - HistoricBatch
    - HistoricDecisionInputInstance
    - HistoricDecisionInstance
    - HistoricDecisionOutputInstance
    - HistoricDetail
    - HistoricExternalTaskLog
    - HistoricIdentityLink
    - HistoricIncident
    - HistoricJobLog
    - HistoricScopeInstance
    - HistoricVariableInstance
    - UserOperationLogEntry
    - Diagram
    - DiagramElement
    - Style
    - BaseElement
    - Definitions
    - Documentation
    - InteractionNode
    - NamedThing
    - ProvenanceBundle
    range: string
    required: true
  name:
    name: name
    description: Human-readable name.
    from_schema: https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform
    related_mappings:
    - stix:name
    - unified_cyber_ontology:name
    rank: 1000
    slot_uri: schema:name
    owner: WorkflowRun
    domain_of:
    - ByteArray
    - MeterLog
    - Property
    - Group
    - Tenant
    - Task
    - VariableInstance
    - Attachment
    - Filter
    - Deployment
    - ResourceDefinition
    - HistoricDetail
    - HistoricTaskInstance
    - HistoricVariableInstance
    - Font
    - Diagram
    - CallableElement
    - Category
    - Collaboration
    - ConversationLink
    - ConversationNode
    - CorrelationKey
    - CorrelationProperty
    - DataInput
    - DataOutput
    - DataState
    - DataStore
    - Definitions
    - Error
    - Escalation
    - FlowElement
    - InputSet
    - Interface
    - Lane
    - LaneSet
    - LinkEventDefinition
    - Message
    - MessageFlow
    - Operation
    - OutputSet
    - Participant
    - BpmnProperty
    - Resource
    - ResourceParameter
    - ResourceRole
    - Signal
    - NamedThing

    range: string
  external_ref:

    domain_of:

    range: string

docs.title: Slot: action
docs.text: The action.

docs.title: Slot: activation_condition
docs.text: Condition that activates this complex gateway.

docs.title: Slot: activity
docs.text: The activity of this element.

docs.title: Slot: activity_id
docs.text: BPMN activity element identifier.

docs.title: Slot: activity_instance_id
docs.text: Runtime activity instance identifier.

docs.title: Slot: activity_instance_state
docs.text: The activity instance state.

docs.title: Slot: activity_name
docs.text: The display name for the activity

docs.title: Slot: activity_type
docs.text: The activity type of the activity. Typically the activity type correspond to the XML tag used in the BPMN 2.0 process definition file. All activity types are available in org.finos.fluxnova.bpm.eng...

docs.title: Slot: agent_kind
docs.text: Kind of agent (person, organization, system, service account).

docs.title: Slot: agents
docs.text: Agents referenced by the bundle.

docs.title: Slot: allowed_values
docs.text: Enumerated allowed values for the parameter, if constrained.

docs.title: Slot: annotation
docs.text: Annotation of this incident

docs.title: Slot: architecture
docs.text: Architecture family (e.g. "transformer-decoder", "moe", "mamba", "diffusion").

docs.title: Slot: arguments_json
docs.text: JSON-serialized arguments passed to the tool.

docs.title: Slot: artifact_kind
docs.text: Classifier for the kind of artifact (BPMN XML, payload, document, log, etc.).

docs.title: Slot: artifacts
docs.text: Artifacts (text annotations, groups, associations) in this process.

docs.title: Slot: assignee
docs.text: The userId of the person to which this task is assigned or delegated.

docs.title: Slot: assignee_hash
docs.text: Hash of the assignee for aggregation.

docs.title: Slot: assigner_id
docs.text: UserId of the user who assigns a task to the user

docs.title: Slot: assignments
docs.text: Data assignments (from/to) within this data association.

docs.title: Slot: association_direction
docs.text: The association direction of this element.

docs.title: Slot: attached_to
docs.text: The activity to which this boundary event is attached.

docs.title: Slot: attempts
docs.text: Number of failed login attempts.

docs.title: Slot: auditing
docs.text: Auditing information attached to this flow element.

docs.title: Slot: authors
docs.text: Authors or owners of the workflow definition (by-reference; declare full agents under ProvenanceBundle.agents).

docs.title: Slot: base_model
docs.text: Upstream base model artifact this artifact derives from.

docs.title: Slot: batch_id
docs.text: Reference to a batch.

docs.title: Slot: batch_job_definition_id
docs.text: Reference to a JobDefinition.

docs.title: Slot: batches
docs.text: Batch operations.

docs.title: Slot: behavior
docs.text: Behavior governing how completion events are thrown.

docs.title: Slot: bold
docs.text: Whether the font is rendered in bold.

docs.title: Slot: bounds
docs.text: Bounding rectangle giving position and size of this diagram element.

docs.title: Slot: bpm_diagrams
docs.text: BPMN diagram elements (BPMNDiagram) in the root definitions.

docs.title: Slot: bpmn_element
docs.text: The BPMN model element this diagram element represents.

docs.title: Slot: bpmn_extension_names
docs.text: Names or identifiers of BPMN extension elements used by the step.

docs.title: Slot: bpmn_label
docs.text: The label element attached to this shape or edge.

docs.title: Slot: bpmn_label_styles
docs.text: Label style definitions available in this BPMN diagram.

docs.title: Slot: bpmn_plane
docs.text: The plane element containing the shapes and edges of this diagram.

docs.title: Slot: bpmn_type
docs.text: Normalized BPMN element type for the step.

docs.title: Slot: business_key
docs.text: Domain-specific business key.

docs.title: Slot: byte_array_id
docs.text: Reference to the byte array.

docs.title: Slot: bytes
docs.text: Serialized binary content.

docs.title: Slot: cached_entity_state
docs.text: Bitmask caching associated entity existence.

docs.title: Slot: called_case_instance_id
docs.text: The called case instance in case of (case) call activity

docs.title: Slot: called_collaboration
docs.text: The collaboration element called by this call conversation.

docs.title: Slot: called_element
docs.text: The global task or process called by this call activity.

docs.title: Slot: called_process_instance_id
docs.text: The called process instance in case of call activity

docs.title: Slot: called_step_run
docs.text: Step run that was materialized to execute the tool call, if any.

docs.title: Slot: capacity
docs.text: Maximum number of items this data store can hold.

docs.title: Slot: case_activity_id
docs.text: The unique identifier of the case activity in the case.

docs.title: Slot: case_activity_name
docs.text: The display name for the case activity.

docs.title: Slot: case_activity_type
docs.text: The display type for the case activity.

docs.title: Slot: case_definition_id
docs.text: Reference to the case definition.

docs.title: Slot: case_definition_key
docs.text: Case definition key reference.

docs.title: Slot: case_execution_id
docs.text: Reference to the case execution.

docs.title: Slot: case_instance_id
docs.text: Reference to the case instance.

docs.title: Slot: category
docs.text: Category classification.

docs.title: Slot: category_value_refs
docs.text: Category values associated with this flow element.

docs.title: Slot: category_values
docs.text: Category values contained in this category.

docs.title: Slot: cause_incident_id
docs.text: Id of the incident on which this incident has been triggered.

docs.title: Slot: child_lane_set
docs.text: The child lane set contained within this lane.

docs.title: Slot: choreography_activity_shape
docs.text: Shape of the associated choreography activity.

docs.title: Slot: classification
docs.text: Data classification of the value (public, internal, confidential, restricted).

docs.title: Slot: clause_id
docs.text: The unique identifier of the clause that the value is assigned for. Can be null if the decision is not implemented as decision table.

docs.title: Slot: clause_name
docs.text: The name of the clause that the value is assigned for. Can be null if the decision is not implemented as decision table.

docs.title: Slot: close_time
docs.text: The time the case was closed.

docs.title: Slot: closed
docs.text: Whether closed.

docs.title: Slot: code
docs.text: Incident or error code.

docs.title: Slot: collect_result_value
docs.text: The result of the collect operation if the hit policy 'collect' was used for the decision.

docs.title: Slot: collection
docs.text: Whether collection.

docs.title: Slot: completion_condition
docs.text: Condition that, when true, terminates remaining instances.

docs.title: Slot: completion_quantity
docs.text: Number of tokens produced when this activity completes.

docs.title: Slot: complex_behavior_definitions
docs.text: Rules defining complex multi-instance completion behavior.

docs.title: Slot: component_kind
docs.text: Kind of runtime component (engine, REST API, executor, plugin, connector, etc.).

docs.title: Slot: condition
docs.text: Condition guard expression.

docs.title: Slot: condition_expression
docs.text: Expression evaluated to decide whether this flow is taken.

docs.title: Slot: configuration
docs.text: Payload of this incident.

docs.title: Slot: configuration_ref
docs.text: Reference to an artifact containing applied configuration.

docs.title: Slot: consumed_artifacts
docs.text: Artifacts consumed by the step run (by-reference; declare full artifacts under ProvenanceBundle.artifacts).

docs.title: Slot: consumed_by_step_runs
docs.text: Step runs that consumed this artifact or parameter value (by-reference).

docs.title: Slot: content
docs.text: Message content (may be redacted in export).

docs.title: Slot: content_hash
docs.text:
Hash of the message content (algorithm-prefixed, e.g. "sha256:...").

Used to deduplicate or to attest to a message that has been redacted.

docs.title: Slot: content_id
docs.text: Reference to the content.

docs.title: Slot: context_window_tokens
docs.text: Maximum input context window in tokens.

docs.title: Slot: conversation_associations
docs.text: Associations linking conversation nodes to other elements.

docs.title: Slot: conversation_links
docs.text: TODO: choreographyRef

docs.title: Slot: conversation_nodes
docs.text: Conversation nodes in this collaboration.

docs.title: Slot: correlation_key
docs.text: Correlation key grouping correlation properties.

docs.title: Slot: correlation_keys
docs.text: Correlation keys used to correlate messages in this collaboration.

docs.title: Slot: correlation_properties
docs.text: Correlation properties defined in this collaboration.

docs.title: Slot: correlation_property
docs.text: The correlation property this binding is based on.

docs.title: Slot: correlation_property_bindings
docs.text: Bindings mapping correlation properties to data paths.

docs.title: Slot: correlation_property_retrieval_expressions
docs.text: Retrieval expressions for this correlation property.

docs.title: Slot: correlation_subscriptions
docs.text: Correlation subscriptions associated with this process.

docs.title: Slot: cost_usd
docs.text:
Direct provider cost in USD for the invocation, where reported by the

provider or computed locally from billing rates.

docs.title: Slot: create_time
docs.text: Creation timestamp.

docs.title: Slot: create_user_id
docs.text: The authenticated user that created this case instance.

docs.title: Slot: created
docs.text: The time this event subscription was created.

docs.title: Slot: current_state
docs.text: The current state.

docs.title: Slot: data_input_associations
docs.text: Data associations that feed input data into this activity.

docs.title: Slot: data_inputs
docs.text: Input data elements of this specification.

docs.title: Slot: data_object
docs.text: The data object that this reference points to.

docs.title: Slot: data_output_associations
docs.text: Data associations that move output data out of this activity.

docs.title: Slot: data_output_refs
docs.text: Produced output data references for this output set.

docs.title: Slot: data_outputs
docs.text: Output data elements of this specification.

docs.title: Slot: data_path
docs.text: XPath expression navigating to the relevant data node.

docs.title: Slot: data_state
docs.text: Current state of this data object or data store reference.

docs.title: Slot: data_store
docs.text: The data store that this reference points to.

docs.title: Slot: dataset_kind
docs.text: Role of the dataset (training, fine-tuning, evaluation, RAG corpus, ground truth).

docs.title: Slot: decision_definition_id
docs.text: The decision definition reference.

docs.title: Slot: decision_definition_key
docs.text: The unique identifier of the decision definition

docs.title: Slot: decision_definition_name
docs.text: The name of the decision definition

docs.title: Slot: decision_instance_id
docs.text: The unique identifier of the historic decision instance.

docs.title: Slot: decision_ref
docs.text: DMN decision or decision service reference for business rule tasks.

docs.title: Slot: decision_requirements_definition_id
docs.text: Id of the related decision requirements definition. Can be null if the decision has no relations to other decisions.

docs.title: Fluxnova Provenance — Design Documents
docs.text: Narrative and reference material for the Fluxnova BPM provenance feature. The canonical, validated schema modules live under src/fluxnova_bpm_platform/schema/provenance/; these documents explain how to populate and project them.

docs.title: Documents
docs.text: File Contents fluxnova_to_linkml_mapping_spec.md Field-level mapping from Fluxnova engine APIs to LinkML classes fluxnova_provenance_rocrate_projection.md Projection of provenance bundles to RO-Crate / JSON-LD fluxnova_ai_provenance_overview.md Per-invocation AI overlay overview and integration guidance

docs.title: Schema modules
docs.text:
The base provenance module is imported by the master platform schema, and the base in turn imports the AI overlay — so both are part of the platform root. The strict overlay is opt-in: a consumer must imports: it explicitly.

Module Imported by master Purpose provenance/fluxnova_bpm_provenance.yaml yes Base PROV-aligned provenance schema provenance/fluxnova_bpm_provenance_strict.yaml no (opt-in) Strict validation overlay (required slots, regex) provenance/fluxnova_bpm_ai_provenance.yaml yes (via base) AI-invocation provenance overlay

The base schema is PROV-aligned and separates prospective provenance (WorkflowDefinition, StepDefinition, ParameterDefinition) from retrospective provenance (WorkflowRun, StepRun, ParameterValue). The strict overlay tightens cardinality and adds identifier/endpoint patterns to make exports deterministic and CI-validatable.

docs.title: Fixtures
docs.text:
• Valid: tests/data/valid/ProvenanceBundle-customer-onboarding.yaml
  • Invalid: tests/data/invalid/ProvenanceBundle-bad-status.yaml

docs.title: Mappings
docs.text: • SSSOM crosswalk to FINOS nexus:: src/fluxnova_bpm_platform/mappings/fluxnova-ai-provenance-to-nexus.sssom.tsv

docs.title: Fluxnova AI Provenance - Overview
docs.text:
The AI provenance overlay layers on top of the generic BPM provenance schema and adds the entities and slots needed to record what happened when a BPMN step invoked an AI model (LLM completion, embedding call, tool use, agent loop, retrieval, or guardrail).

It is an opt-in overlay - neither the strict overlay nor the AI overlay is imported by fluxnova_bpm_platform.yaml. Consumers imports: it explicitly only when they need it.

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• Schema: src/fluxnova_bpm_platform/schema/provenance/fluxnova_bpm_ai_provenance.yaml
  • SSSOM crosswalk: src/fluxnova_bpm_platform/mappings/fluxnova-ai-provenance-to-nexus.sssom.tsv

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
The overlay is retrospective per-invocation. It records concrete runtime evidence:

  • Which AI agent / model was called by a workflow step
  • Conversation messages and roles
  • Tool calls made inside an agent loop
  • Token / cost telemetry
  • Safety / guardrail decisions
  • Online evaluation results (LLM-as-judge, metric, guardrail, human review)
  • Datasets and model artifacts referenced by the invocation

It deliberately does not cover prospective AI governance modeling (what an AI system is, what risks/controls apply, benchmark cards, risk taxonomies). That space is owned upstream by FINOS ai-governance-framework.

docs.title: Class summary
docs.text: Overlay class is_a Purpose AiAgent Agent Concrete deployed AI agent / model endpoint AiModelDescriptor RuntimeComponent Model family, architecture, parameter count, modalities AiModelInvocation StepRun One invocation of a model from a BPMN step PromptMessage NamedThing One turn in the conversation (system/user/assistant/tool) ToolCall NamedThing A tool/function call inside an agent loop EvaluationResult NamedThing Online quality measurement of an invocation AiDataset WorkflowArtifact Dataset artifact (training, fine-tuning, eval, RAG, GT) ModelArtifact WorkflowArtifact Persistent model artifact (checkpoint, weights, card)

docs.title: Enums
docs.text: MessageRole, AiModality, AiInvocationKind, SafetyFlag, DatasetKind, EvaluatorKind - each permissible value carries a description: and a meaning: mapping where a stable upstream URI exists (per the Do not guess mappings rule in the LinkML SKILL).

docs.title: Mapping strategy
docs.text:
The overlay follows the project's "own-prefix identity, upstream in mappings" convention:

  • class_uri: and slot_uri: always point at the overlay's own w3id-resolvable IRI (fluxnova_bpm_ai_provenance:*).
  • Upstream vocabulary CURIEs go into exact_mappings, close_mappings, or related_mappings on the relevant element.
  • Cross-vocabulary mappings to FINOS nexus: (AI Governance Framework ontology), W3C prov:, airo:, dpv:, dpv_ai:, dpv_risk:, dqv:, mls:, cr: (MLCommons Croissant), and schema: are declared inline AND in the standalone SSSOM TSV crosswalk for externally-maintainable governance tooling.
  • The overlay does not imports: ai-risk-ontology. A separate governance-bridge overlay can be added later if a real consumer asks for native AIRO/DPV-AI type checking; until then SSSOM is sufficient.

docs.title: Sample usage
docs.text:
The overlay is consumed by adding:

imports:
  - linkml:types
  - ./fluxnova_common
  - ./provenance/fluxnova_bpm_provenance
  - ./provenance/fluxnova_bpm_ai_provenance

to a downstream schema, then instantiating AiModelInvocation records inside a ProvenanceBundle exactly as you would StepRun records (since AiModelInvocation is_a StepRun and the overlay's classes are not added to the master platform schema).

docs.title: Related vocabularies
docs.text:
• W3C PROV-O - prov:Activity, prov:Entity, prov:Agent, prov:wasInformedBy, prov:wasGeneratedBy
  • W3C DQV - dqv:QualityMeasurement for EvaluationResult
  • W3C AIRO - airo:AIAgent, airo:AIModel, airo:Modality
  • W3C DPV-AI and DPV-RISK - risk taxonomy for safety flags
  • W3ID MLS - machine-learning schema (mls:ModelEvaluation)
  • MLCommons Croissant - cr:Dataset
  • FINOS AI Governance Framework nexus: - governance and risk-control concepts

docs.title: RO-Crate / JSON-LD Projection for Fluxnova Provenance
Purpose
docs.text: This document describes how the Fluxnova provenance LinkML model can be projected into a JSON-LD / RO-Crate style representation.

docs.title: Projection strategy
docs.text: The LinkML model is the canonical authoring/validation layer. JSON-LD / RO-Crate is the interchange and graph-publication layer.

docs.title: Core projection rules
docs.text:
• ProvenanceBundle -> RO-Crate root dataset / top-level crate container
  • WorkflowDefinition -> JSON-LD node with BPMN / computational workflow semantics
  • StepDefinition -> prospective step/method node
  • WorkflowRun -> retrospective run action
  • StepRun -> retrospective step execution action
  • ParameterDefinition -> formal parameter node
  • ParameterValue -> actual value node
  • Agent, Environment, RuntimeComponent, WorkflowArtifact -> contextual or data entities

docs.title: Recommended JSON-LD context sketch
docs.text:
{
  "@context": {
    "schema": "http://schema.org/",
    "prov": "http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#",
    "fluxnova_bpm_provenance": "https://w3id.org/lmodel/fluxnova-bpm-platform/provenance/",
    "id": "@id",
    "type": "@type",
    "name": "schema:name",
    "description": "schema:description",
    "instrument": {"@id": "schema:instrument", "@type": "@id"},
    "object": {"@id": "schema:object", "@type": "@id"},
    "result": {"@id": "schema:result", "@type": "@id"},
    "agent": {"@id": "schema:agent", "@type": "@id"},
    "startTime": "schema:startTime",
    "endTime": "schema:endTime",
    "exampleOfWork": {"@id": "schema:exampleOfWork", "@type": "@id"},
    "workExample": {"@id": "schema:workExample", "@type": "@id"},
    "used": {"@id": "prov:used", "@type": "@id"},
    "wasGeneratedBy": {"@id": "prov:wasGeneratedBy", "@type": "@id"}
  }
}

docs.title: Root entities
docs.text:
• Metadata descriptor: ro-crate-metadata.json
  • Root data entity: ./
  • Crate metadata includes workflow_definitions, workflow_runs, workflow_artifacts

docs.title: Prospective entities
docs.text:
• Workflow definition node as File + SoftwareSourceCode + ComputationalWorkflow + HowTo when exporting BPMN model artifacts
  • Step definitions as HowToStep and/or SoftwareSourceCode style nodes depending on desired fidelity
  • Parameter definitions as FormalParameter

docs.title: Retrospective entities
docs.text:
• Workflow run as CreateAction
  • Step run as CreateAction
  • Optional orchestration node as OrganizeAction
  • Optional step-linking node as ControlAction
  • Actual values as PropertyValue

docs.title: Minimal example projection
docs.text:
{
  "@context": "https://w3id.org/ro/crate/1.1/context",
  "@graph": [
    {
      "@id": "ro-crate-metadata.json",
      "@type": "CreativeWork",
      "about": {"@id": "./"}
    },
    {
      "@id": "./",
      "@type": "Dataset",
      "name": "Fluxnova provenance bundle"
    },
    {
      "@id": "wfdef:customer-onboarding-review:1",
      "@type": ["File", "SoftwareSourceCode", "ComputationalWorkflow", "HowTo"],
      "name": "Customer Onboarding Review",
      "step": [{"@id": "stepdef:intake-service-task"}]
    },
    {
      "@id": "stepdef:intake-service-task",
      "@type": "HowToStep",
      "name": "Capture Intake Data"
    },
    {
      "@id": "wfrun:customer-onboarding-review:90001",
      "@type": "CreateAction",
      "instrument": {"@id": "wfdef:customer-onboarding-review:1"},
      "startTime": "2026-06-11T09:00:00+01:00",
      "endTime": "2026-06-11T09:08:45+01:00"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Export recommendation
docs.text:
A practical export pipeline is:

  1. Export Fluxnova runtime/history into LinkML instance YAML/JSON.
  2. Validate against the strict LinkML profile.
  3. Transform validated instance data into JSON-LD / RO-Crate using deterministic IDs.
  4. Load JSON-LD into graph tooling or publish crate bundles for downstream analysis.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This mapping specification describes how Fluxnova engine/runtime concepts should populate the Fluxnova provenance LinkML model.

docs.title: Mapping principles
docs.text:
1. Prefer stable engine identifiers for definitions and runtime instances.
  2. Preserve definition vs execution separation.
  3. Preserve formal definition vs actual observed value separation.
  4. Export references to large payloads as WorkflowArtifact where direct embedding is undesirable.

docs.title: Definition layer
docs.text:
• BPMN process definition key / deployed process definition -> WorkflowDefinition.id, WorkflowDefinition.definition_key, WorkflowDefinition.version, WorkflowDefinition.deployment_id
  • BPMN XML model artifact -> WorkflowArtifact with artifact_kind: BPMN_XML; referenced from WorkflowDefinition.source_model_ref
  • BPMN flow node / task / event / gateway -> StepDefinition
  • Task implementation metadata (delegate class, connector id, called element, script reference, expression, form key, DMN ref) -> StepDefinition.implementation_ref, called_element, decision_ref, form_ref, implementation_kind
  • Input/output variable contracts -> ParameterDefinition

docs.title: Runtime layer
docs.text:
• Process instance / historic process instance -> WorkflowRun
  • Activity instance / task execution -> StepRun
  • Runtime variable snapshot / variable history record -> ParameterValue
  • Incident / failed job / retry metadata -> ProvenanceIncident, StepRun.retries, StepRun.incident_message
  • Execution timing / duration / queue wait / retry counts -> ResourceUsage
  • Deployed environment / engine endpoint / plugin identity -> Environment, RuntimeComponent
  • External payloads, documents, or serialized request/response bodies -> WorkflowArtifact

docs.title: WorkflowDefinition
docs.text:
• id: deterministic URI/CURIE built from process definition key + version
  • external_ref: raw engine identifier (if different from schema id)
  • definition_key: BPMN process key
  • version: deployed version tag/version field
  • deployment_id: engine deployment identifier
  • source_model_ref: BPMN artifact
  • runtime_component: engine component that executes the definition
  • steps: all BPMN executable flow nodes and relevant events/gateways

docs.title: StepDefinition
docs.text:
• id: deterministic URI/CURIE built from workflow definition + BPMN element id
  • workflow_definition: owning process definition
  • bpmn_type: normalized BPMN element type enum
  • implementation_kind: normalized implementation enum
  • implementation_ref: Java delegate class, expression, script path, connector id, etc.
  • input_parameters / output_parameters: formal variable contracts or IO mappings where available

docs.title: WorkflowRun
docs.text:
• id: deterministic URI/CURIE built from process instance id
  • workflow_definition: owning definition id
  • business_key: engine business key/correlation key
  • status: normalized status enum
  • started_at / ended_at: process timestamps
  • started_by: initiator principal if available
  • environment: cluster/runtime environment
  • runtime_component: engine component
  • input_values: process-start variables and other top-level inputs
  • output_values: terminal or completion outputs
  • step_runs: all activity/task executions in scope

docs.title: StepRun
docs.text:
• id: deterministic URI/CURIE built from activity instance id
  • workflow_run: owning process instance
  • step_definition: BPMN element definition
  • activity_instance_id: native activity instance id
  • status: normalized status enum
  • started_at / ended_at: activity timestamps
  • executed_by: assignee/system principal where meaningful
  • execution_resource: resource/pod/worker node if meaningful
  • input_values: values consumed at step boundary
  • output_values: values produced at step boundary
  • consumed_artifacts / produced_artifacts: external payloads / documents / logs / exports
  • retries: retry count for retriable execution
  • sequence_no: exporter-assigned ordering field for deterministic serialization

docs.title: ParameterDefinition / ParameterValue
docs.text:
• ParameterDefinition.id: definition-scoped ID for a variable contract
  • ParameterDefinition.parameter_scope: WORKFLOW / STEP / TASK / DECISION / FORM
  • ParameterDefinition.direction: IN / OUT / INOUT / LOCAL
  • ParameterValue.parameter_definition: link to formal definition
  • ParameterValue.observed_type: runtime-observed concrete type
  • ParameterValue.serialized_value: export-safe representation (truncate or externalize large values if needed)
  • ParameterValue.value_hash: stable digest for dedupe / comparison / diffing
  • ParameterValue.produced_by_step_run and consumed_by_step_runs: optional lineage edges

docs.title: ID strategy recommendation
docs.text:
Use deterministic IDs rather than exporter-local counters.

Examples:

  • wfdef:{process_key}:{version}
  • stepdef:{process_key}:{bpmn_element_id}
  • wfrun:{process_instance_id}
  • steprun:{activity_instance_id}
  • paramdef:{scope}:{qualified_name}
  • pval:{run_id}:{qualified_name}:{sequence_no}

docs.title: Redaction recommendation
docs.text:
For sensitive variables:

  • keep ParameterDefinition.secret: true
  • set ParameterValue.redacted: true
  • store only value_hash and a policy-safe serialized_value placeholder
  • externalize raw payloads to controlled stores and reference them as WorkflowArtifact
